summary: Ukrainian drones are increasingly striking rear areas at distances of 40+ km, with loitering munitions now appearing up to 100 km from the front line, indicating improved range and payload capacity. The enemy is combining mass-produced cheap drones with expensive foreign models, rapidly scaling up production of systems capable of hitting moving vehicles. Meanwhile, Russian forces are countering with improved electronic warfare, including FPV control frequency reduction and larger drone antennas, while also using motorcycles for assaults and deploying drone detectors to anticipate attacks.
